content.full_text:
A-78-dS-7

March 14, 1978


Honorable William F. Moreno
Presiding Judge, Salinas Municipal Court
Post Office Box 1409
Salinas, California 93901

Dear Mr. Moreno:

Thank you for your letter of January 23, 1978. Please accept my
apologies for our delay in answering your question.

Your assumption is correct. I refer you to Government Code Section
34206, last sentence. If in fact you were not a candidate in 1977
or 1978 and did not receive contributions or make expenditures during
the 1977 semi-annual periods, there was no obligation on your part to
file semi-annual statements for these periods.

Sincerely,


John D. Giansello
Political Reform Analyst

MONTEREY COUNTY

MUNICIPAL COURT -SALINAS JUDICIAL DISTRICT

(406) 424-8611, P.O. BOX 1409 - SALINAS, CALIFORNIA 93901

WILLIAM F. MORENO

RAYMOND H. SIMMONS

ROBERT O'FARRELL
JUDGES OF MUNICIPAL COURT

ROBERT DOWNS
CLERK OF MUNICIPAL COURT

January 23, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

Gentlemen:

I have examined the most recent literature with reference to campaign
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. I am a Municipal Court Judge
for the Salinas Judicial District, County of Monterey. This is not a year in
which I am engaged in an election campaign, and I further certify that I have
not received, nor have I spent, any amount of money.

I find it exceedingly difficult to make heads or tails out of all the infor-
mation requested. However, it seems to me that I would come under the des-
cription of an elected office holder in an "off year", and, as a judge, not
required to file semi-annual campaign statements. If I am incorrect, could you
please forward to me the form so that I can tell you that I did not receive any
money nor expend any money.

Thank you kindly for your courtesy and cooperation.

Very truly yours,


William F. Moreno, Presiding Judge
Salinas Municipal Court

dl

sections.question: This is not a year in which I am engaged in an election campaign, and I further certify that I have not received, nor have I spent, any amount of money. If I am incorrect, could you please forward to me the form so that I can tell you that I did not receive any money nor expend any money.
sections.question_synthetic: As a Municipal Court Judge in an off-election year who has received and spent no campaign funds, is the judge required to file semi-annual campaign statements under the Political Reform Act?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a judge to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present. The letter contains the substantive inquiry and request for guidance. Letter dated January 23, 1978.

embedding.summary: A Municipal Court Judge for the Salinas Judicial District writes to the FPPC seeking clarification on whether judges are required to file campaign disclosure statements in off-election years, stating they believe they are exempt as an elected official in an off-year with no campaign activity or funds.
